skill.name: gws-gmail-watch
skill.description: Watch Gmail for new messages with the Google Workspace CLI and stream matching email events as NDJSON.
skill.summary: Monitor Gmail inbox changes and stream new messages for automation workflows.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution, path-traversal, and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives in Markdown documentation, command examples, and relative documentation links. The real risk is operational: the documented command monitors Gmail, uses Google Pub/Sub resources, and can write message content to local files.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Gmail Message Streaming
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents watching Gmail and streaming new emails as NDJSON. This can expose personal or business message content if used with broad labels, shared terminals, logs, or downstream processors.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented purpose is to stream Gmail messages, and the flags control message filters, formats, and file output. This is a clear data exposure risk, but it is expected functionality rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Google Pub/Sub Resource Use
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The command can create or use Pub/Sub topics and subscriptions for Gmail push notifications. Incorrect project or cleanup choices can leave resources active after the workflow ends.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flags and tips explicitly reference GCP project, Pub/Sub resources, cleanup, and persistence. The risk depends on user configuration and permissions.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged shell execution, but the evidence is Markdown documentation showing how a user may run the gws CLI. The skill file does not contain executable script code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced bash examples in documentation. No Ruby backtick execution or dynamic command construction appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The ../ sequences point to sibling skill documentation files. They are static Markdown links, not runtime file access with user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are prerequisite and see-also links in Markdown. There is no evidence of filesystem traversal at runtime.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Has No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm alerts at lines 3 and 28 do not correspond to cryptographic code. Line 3 is a description, and line 28 is a table header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text and Markdown table labels only. No hash, cipher, signature, or password handling code is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Local Email File Output
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The --output-dir flag writes each message to a separate JSON file. Users should choose protected directories and avoid storing sensitive mail in shared workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flag description and example explicitly show message files being written locally. The risk is data persistence, not arbitrary file write evidence.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable way to react when important Gmail messages arrive. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the GWS Gmail watch workflow.
actual_capabilities: Shows the basic command for starting a Gmail watch stream.
Documents filters for labels such as INBOX and UNREAD.
Explains message format options for full, metadata, minimal, and raw output.
Covers Pub/Sub project, topic, and subscription options.
Shows one-time pulls, cleanup behavior, and local message file output.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI and the shared Google Workspace authentication setup.
Requires Gmail and Google Cloud permissions for the selected account and project.
Does not define parsing, alerting, or downstream automation logic.
Gmail watches expire after seven days and must be renewed.

use_cases.title: Inbox Triage Automation
use_cases.description: Monitor selected Gmail labels and stream new messages for review or routing workflows.

use_cases.title: Support Queue Monitoring
use_cases.description: Pull new support emails once or continuously while preserving control over batch size.

use_cases.title: Email Data Collection
use_cases.description: Write received messages to local files for controlled analysis or archival workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Watch
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me run a basic Gmail watch with gws. Explain the required authentication and the simplest command.

prompt_templates.title: Watch a Specific Label
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a gws gmail watch command that monitors only INBOX unread messages and pulls one batch.

prompt_templates.title: Use Existing Pub/Sub Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me use an existing Pub/Sub subscription for Gmail watch without creating new resources.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Safe Email Capture Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a secure Gmail watch workflow that writes message files locally, limits labels, and cleans up temporary resources.

output_examples.input: User asks how to watch Gmail once for unread inbox messages.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a one-time watch using the INBOX and UNREAD labels, then explains expected streaming output.

output_examples.input: User asks how to keep Pub/Sub resources after stopping the watch.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that resources persist unless cleanup is requested, and advises documenting ownership and renewal timing.

output_examples.input: User asks how to store watched messages safely.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a protected output directory, limited labels, minimal retention, and careful handling of message files.

best_practices: Use narrow label filters before streaming message data.
Use protected output directories when saving email files.
Clean up temporary Pub/Sub resources when they are no longer needed.
anti_patterns: Do not stream all mailbox data when a narrow label filter is enough.
Do not write Gmail message files into shared or public folders.
Do not leave temporary Pub/Sub resources unmanaged after testing.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps run gws commands that watch Gmail for new messages and stream results as NDJSON.

faq.question: Does it require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the shared Google Workspace authentication setup referenced by the skill.

faq.question: Can it filter which messages are watched?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented label IDs option can filter messages by labels such as INBOX or UNREAD.

faq.question: Can it save messages to files?
faq.answer: Yes. The output directory option writes each message to a separate JSON file.

faq.question: Does the Gmail watch run forever?
faq.answer: No. The skill notes that Gmail watch expires after seven days and must be renewed.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive email?
faq.answer: Use caution. Limit labels, protect local output files, and avoid sending streamed data to untrusted systems.
